danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring player evasion or elimination

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy unit patterns and formations across stages
danger_signals.meaning: Organized military opposition in fictional African country of Zutula

danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming bullets and explosions from enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate damage source; vitality gauge depletion indicator

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar visual feedback during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Player character taking cumulative damage; proximity to game-over state
High-damage threat requiring tactical response or vehicle hijacking

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible item dropped by defeated enemies or found in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Vitality restoration; player health recovery mechanic

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating collectibles that increase maximum vitality
safety_signals.meaning: Permanent stat improvement; enhanced survival capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Equipment drops (Assault Rifle, Shotgun, Grenade Launcher, Flamethrower)
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced offensive capability; tactical advantage shift
Screen-clear emergency tool; temporary invulnerability state

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy vehicles available for player hijacking in specific stages
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced protection and offensive capability transition

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multi-color (Player 1: Blue, Player 2: Red, Player 3: Yellow)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Playable character appearance; control over protagonist movement and actions

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Soldier types, vehicle operators, varied enemy formations across stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy deployment patterns; stage-specific rebel strongholds and fortifications; Hercules Transport as faction asset

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Implied through rescue objective (former President extraction)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Government infrastructure; presidential location as final stage objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy forces present across all six stages; combat-heavy environments; fortified locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy spawning; organized rebel unit formations; escalating difficulty through stage progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final stage features Hercules Transport; president location as level endpoint
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rescue objective drives player progression; final confrontation narrative structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player team composition (three distinct character colors); infiltration-focused stage design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player agency through weapon selection and tactical movement; multi-character option (Genesis version)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six numbered stages indicating geographic progression through rebel-held areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Linear stage-based advancement; enemy presence increases with territorial penetration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Currency system; Weapon Shop locations integrated into stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manage defeated-enemy currency drops; strategic weapon purchasing system

environmental_storytelling.example: Rescue former President from rebels in fictional African country of Zutula; final stage features Hercules Transport as literal extraction point
Six sequential stages represent mercenary team advancing through rebel-held territory toward presidential location
Different soldier formations and vehicle encounters across stages indicate escalating rebel resistance and fortification levels
Certain stages allow player hijacking of enemy vehicles, indicating captured military assets and tactical advantage shifts
Genesis version offers multiple characters (Blue, Red, Yellow) each characterized by weapon specialization, indicating tactical role differentiation
Weapon Shops in stages suggest functional Zutula economy; currency earned from defeated enemies implies resource competition narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active combat engagement; projectile fire toward player; movement patterns across stage
Item drop (First-Aid Kit, power-up, Megacrush bomb, or currency in Original Mode); removal from stage
Full movement range; weapon firing; stage progression capability
Game-over state triggered; stage reset required
Enhanced offensive output (Assault Rifle → Shotgun/Grenade Launcher/Flamethrower); tactical approach modification
Mobile threat; high damage output; stage-specific appearance
Enhanced player defensive/offensive capability; increased survivability; stage-specific tactical advantage
Instant screen-clear activation; all on-screen enemies eliminated; limited-use resource management
Passive rescue target; end-stage objective trigger; narrative completion marker

notes: Mercs communicates world-state primarily through mechanical progression (stage-based enemy escalation) and objective clarity rather than environmental visual complexity. The three-player color-coding system (Blue/Red/Yellow) creates immediate faction identity for player characters. Genesis version introduces economy mechanic (currency/Weapon Shops) as world-state indicator, suggesting resource-constrained conflict environment. Political situation (apartheid government, rebel forces, presidential rescue) is textual/narrative rather than visually manifested in available documentation. Danger signals rely heavily on traditional shmup/run-and-gun conventions: enemy presence, projectile fire, vitality gauge. Safety signals are item-based rather than environmental. The shift from Arcade Mode to Genesis Original Mode represents different world-state framing: rescue mission vs. resource-management conflict scenario.
